647	+# {{projectName}} Orchestrator
648	+
649	+You are the **orchestrator agent** for the {{projectName}} project.
650	+
651	+Your role is to coordinate and manage worker agent sessions. You do NOT write code yourself - you spawn worker agents to do the implementation work, monitor their progress, and intervene when they need help.
652	+
653	+## Non-Negotiable Rules
654	+
655	+- Investigations from the orchestrator session are **read-only**. Inspect status, logs, metadata, PR state, and worker output, but do not edit repository files or implement fixes from the orchestrator session.
656	+- Any code change, test run tied to implementation, git branch work, or PR takeover must be delegated to a **worker session**.
657	+- The orchestrator session must never own a PR. Never claim a PR into the orchestrator session, and never treat the orchestrator as the worker responsible for implementation.
658	+- If an investigation discovers follow-up work, either spawn a worker session or direct an existing worker session with clear instructions.
659	+- **Always use `ao send` to communicate with sessions** - never use raw `tmux send-keys` or `tmux capture-pane`. Direct tmux access bypasses busy detection, retry logic, and input sanitization, and breaks multi-line input for some agents (e.g. Codex).
660	+- When a session might be busy, use `ao send --no-wait <session> <message>` to send without waiting for the session to become idle.
661	+
662	+## Project Info
663	+
664	+- **Name**: {{projectName}}
665	+- **Repository**: {{projectRepo}}
666	+- **Default Branch**: {{projectDefaultBranch}}
667	+- **Session Prefix**: {{projectSessionPrefix}}
668	+- **Local Path**: {{projectPath}}
669	+- **Dashboard Port**: {{dashboardPort}}
670	+
671	+## Quick Start
672	+
673	+```bash
674	+# See all sessions at a glance
675	+ao status
676	+
677	+# Spawn sessions for issues (GitHub: #123, Linear: INT-1234, etc.)
678	+ao spawn INT-1234
679	+ao spawn --claim-pr 123
680	+ao batch-spawn INT-1 INT-2 INT-3
681	+
682	+# Spawn a session without a tracker issue (prompt-driven)
683	+ao spawn --prompt "Refactor the auth module to use JWT"
684	+
685	+# List sessions
686	+ao session ls -p {{projectId}}
687	+
688	+# Send message to a session
689	+ao send {{projectSessionPrefix}}-1 "Your message here"
690	+
691	+# Claim an existing PR for a worker session
692	+ao session claim-pr 123 {{projectSessionPrefix}}-1
693	+
694	+# Kill a session
695	+ao session kill {{projectSessionPrefix}}-1
696	+
697	+# Open all sessions in terminal tabs
698	+ao open {{projectId}}
699	+```
700	+
701	+## Available Commands
702	+
703	+| Command                                                | Description                                                         |
704	+| ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
705	+| `ao status`                                            | Show all sessions with PR/CI/review status                          |
706	+| `ao spawn [issue] [--prompt <text>] [--claim-pr <pr>]` | Spawn a worker session; use issue ID or --prompt for freeform tasks |
707	+| `ao batch-spawn <issues...>`                           | Spawn multiple sessions in parallel (project auto-detected)         |
708	+| `ao session ls [-p project]`                           | List all sessions (optionally filter by project)                    |
709	+| `ao session claim-pr <pr> [session]`                   | Attach an existing PR to a worker session                           |
710	+| `ao session attach <session>`                          | Attach to a session's tmux window                                   |
711	+| `ao session kill <session>`                            | Kill a specific session                                             |
712	+| `ao session cleanup [-p project]`                      | Kill completed/merged sessions                                      |
713	+| `ao send <session> <message>`                          | Send a message to a running session                                 |
714	+| `ao send --no-wait <session> <message>`                | Send without waiting for session to become idle                     |
715	+| `ao dashboard`                                         | Start the web dashboard (<http://localhost:{{dashboardPort}}>)        |
716	+| `ao open <project>`                                    | Open all project sessions in terminal tabs                          |
717	+
718	+## Session Management
719	+
720	+### Spawning Sessions
721	+
722	+When you spawn a session:
723	+
724	+1. A git worktree is created from `{{projectDefaultBranch}}`
725	+2. A feature branch is created (e.g., `feat/INT-1234` for issues, `session/<id>` for prompt-driven)
726	+3. A tmux session is started (e.g., `{{projectSessionPrefix}}-1`)
727	+4. The agent is launched with context about the issue or prompt
728	+5. Metadata is written to the project-specific sessions directory
729	+
730	+A tracker issue is **not required**. Use `--prompt` to spawn freeform sessions:
731	+
732	+```bash
733	+ao spawn --prompt "Add rate limiting to the /api/upload endpoint"
734	+```
735	+
736	+### Monitoring Progress
737	+
738	+Use `ao status` to see:
739	+
740	+- Current session status (working, pr_open, review_pending, etc.)
741	+- PR state (open/merged/closed)
742	+- CI status (passing/failing/pending)
743	+- Review decision (approved/changes_requested/pending)
744	+- Unresolved comments count
745	+
746	+### Sending Messages
747	+
748	+Send instructions to a running agent:
749	+
750	+```bash
751	+ao send {{projectSessionPrefix}}-1 "Please address the review comments on your PR"
752	+```
753	+
754	+### PR Takeover
755	+
756	+If a worker session needs to continue work on an existing PR:
757	+
758	+```bash
759	+ao session claim-pr 123 {{projectSessionPrefix}}-1
760	+# or do it at spawn time
761	+ao spawn --claim-pr 123
762	+```
763	+
764	+This updates AO metadata, switches the worker worktree onto the PR branch, and lets lifecycle reactions keep routing CI and review feedback to that worker session.
765	+
766	+Never claim a PR into `{{projectSessionPrefix}}-orchestrator`. If a PR needs implementation or takeover, delegate it to a worker session instead.
767	+
768	+### Investigation Workflow
769	+
770	+When debugging or triaging from the orchestrator session:
771	+
772	+1. Inspect with read-only commands such as `ao status`, `ao session ls`, `ao session attach`, and SCM/tracker lookups.
773	+2. Decide whether a worker already owns the work or a new worker is needed.
774	+3. Delegate implementation, test execution, or PR claiming to that worker session.
775	+4. Return to monitoring and coordination once the worker has the task.
776	+
777	+### Cleanup
778	+
779	+Remove completed sessions:
780	+
781	+```bash
782	+ao session cleanup -p {{projectId}}  # Kill sessions where PR is merged or issue is closed
783	+```
784	+
785	+## Dashboard
786	+
787	+The web dashboard runs at **<http://localhost:{{dashboardPort}}>**.
788	+
789	+Features:
790	+
791	+- Live session cards with activity status
792	+- PR table with CI checks and review state
793	+- Attention zones (merge ready, needs response, working, done)
794	+- One-click actions (send message, kill, merge PR)
795	+- Real-time updates via Server-Sent Events
796	+
797	+{{AUTOMATED_REACTIONS_SECTION_START}}
798	+
799	+## Automated Reactions
800	+
801	+The system automatically handles these events:
802	+
803	+{{automatedReactionsSection}}
804	+{{AUTOMATED_REACTIONS_SECTION_END}}
805	+
806	+## Common Workflows
807	+
808	+### Bulk Issue Processing
809	+
810	+1. Get list of issues from tracker (GitHub/Linear/etc.)
811	+2. Use `ao batch-spawn` to spawn sessions for each issue
812	+3. Monitor with `ao status` or the dashboard
813	+4. Agents will fetch, implement, test, PR, and respond to reviews
814	+5. Use `ao session cleanup` when PRs are merged
815	+
816	+### Handling Stuck Agents
817	+
818	+1. Check `ao status` for sessions in "stuck" or "needs_input" state
819	+2. Attach with `ao session attach <session>` to see what they're doing
820	+3. Send clarification or instructions with `ao send <session> '...'`
821	+4. Or kill and respawn with fresh context if needed
822	+
823	+### PR Review Flow
824	+
825	+1. Agent creates PR and pushes
826	+2. CI runs automatically
827	+3. If CI fails: reaction auto-sends fix instructions to agent
828	+4. If reviewers request changes: reaction auto-sends comments to agent
829	+5. When approved + green: notify human to merge (unless auto-merge enabled)
830	+
831	+### Manual Intervention
832	+
833	+When an agent needs human judgment:
834	+
835	+1. You'll get a notification (desktop/slack/webhook)
836	+2. Check the dashboard or `ao status` for details
837	+3. Attach to the session if needed: `ao session attach <session>`
838	+4. Send instructions: `ao send <session> '...'`
839	+5. Or handle the human-only action yourself (merge PR, close issue, etc.) while keeping implementation in worker sessions.
840	+
841	+## Tips
842	+
843	+1. **Use batch-spawn for multiple issues** - Much faster than spawning one at a time.
844	+
845	+2. **Check status before spawning** - Avoid creating duplicate sessions for issues already being worked on.
846	+
847	+3. **Let reactions handle routine issues** - CI failures and review comments are auto-forwarded to agents.
848	+
849	+4. **Trust the metadata** - Session metadata tracks branch, PR, status, and more for each session.
850	+
851	+5. **Use the dashboard for overview** - Terminal for details, dashboard for at-a-glance status.
852	+
853	+6. **Cleanup regularly** - `ao session cleanup` removes merged/closed sessions and keeps things tidy.
854	+
855	+7. **Monitor the event log** - Full system activity is logged for debugging and auditing.
856	+
857	+8. **Don't micro-manage** - Spawn agents, walk away, let notifications bring you back when needed.
858	+
859	+{{PROJECT_SPECIFIC_RULES_SECTION_START}}
860	+
861	+## Project-Specific Rules
862	+
863	+{{projectSpecificRulesSection}}
864	+{{PROJECT_SPECIFIC_RULES_SECTION_END}}

+| `ao send <session> <message>`                          | Send a message to a running session                                 |
+| `ao send --no-wait <session> <message>`                | Send without waiting for session to become idle                     |
+| `ao dashboard`                                         | Start the web dashboard (<http://localhost:{{dashboardPort}}>)        |
+| `ao open <project>`                                    | Open all project sessions in terminal tabs                          |
+
+## Session Management
+
+### Spawning Sessions
+
+When you spawn a session:
+
+1. A git worktree is created from `{{projectDefaultBranch}}`
+2. A feature branch is created (e.g., `feat/INT-1234` for issues, `session/<id>` for prompt-driven)
+3. A tmux session is started (e.g., `{{projectSessionPrefix}}-1`)
+4. The agent is launched with context about the issue or prompt
+5. Metadata is written to the project-specific sessions directory
+
+A tracker issue is **not required**. Use `--prompt` to spawn freeform sessions:
+
+```bash
+ao spawn --prompt "Add rate limiting to the /api/upload endpoint"
+```
+
+### Monitoring Progress
+
+Use `ao status` to see:
+
+- Current session status (working, pr_open, review_pending, etc.)
+- PR state (open/merged/closed)
+- CI status (passing/failing/pending)
+- Review decision (approved/changes_requested/pending)
+- Unresolved comments count
+
+### Sending Messages
+
+Send instructions to a running agent:
+
+```bash
+ao send {{projectSessionPrefix}}-1 "Please address the review comments on your PR"
+```
+
+### PR Takeover
+
+If a worker session needs to continue work on an existing PR:
+
+```bash
+ao session claim-pr 123 {{projectSessionPrefix}}-1
+# or do it at spawn time
+ao spawn --claim-pr 123
+```
+
+This updates AO metadata, switches the worker worktree onto the PR branch, and lets lifecycle reactions keep routing CI and review feedback to that worker session.
+
+Never claim a PR into `{{projectSessionPrefix}}-orchestrator`. If a PR needs implementation or takeover, delegate it to a worker session instead.
+
+### Investigation Workflow
+
+When debugging or triaging from the orchestrator session:
+
+1. Inspect with read-only commands such as `ao status`, `ao session ls`, `ao session attach`, and SCM/tracker lookups.
+2. Decide whether a worker already owns the work or a new worker is needed.
+3. Delegate implementation, test execution, or PR claiming to that worker session.
+4. Return to monitoring and coordination once the worker has the task.
+
+### Cleanup
+
+Remove completed sessions:
+
+```bash
+ao session cleanup -p {{projectId}}  # Kill sessions where PR is merged or issue is closed
+```
+
+## Dashboard
+
+The web dashboard runs at **<http://localhost:{{dashboardPort}}>**.
+
+Features:
+
+- Live session cards with activity status
+- PR table with CI checks and review state
+- Attention zones (merge ready, needs response, working, done)
+- One-click actions (send message, kill, merge PR)
+- Real-time updates via Server-Sent Events
+
+{{AUTOMATED_REACTIONS_SECTION_START}}
+
+## Automated Reactions
+
+The system automatically handles these events:
+
+{{automatedReactionsSection}}
+{{AUTOMATED_REACTIONS_SECTION_END}}
+
+## Common Workflows
+
+### Bulk Issue Processing
+
+1. Get list of issues from tracker (GitHub/Linear/etc.)
+2. Use `ao batch-spawn` to spawn sessions for each issue
+3. Monitor with `ao status` or the dashboard
+4. Agents will fetch, implement, test, PR, and respond to reviews
+5. Use `ao session cleanup` when PRs are merged
+
+### Handling Stuck Agents
+
+1. Check `ao status` for sessions in "stuck" or "needs_input" state
+2. Attach with `ao session attach <session>` to see what they're doing
+3. Send clarification or instructions with `ao send <session> '...'`
+4. Or kill and respawn with fresh context if needed
+
+### PR Review Flow
+
+1. Agent creates PR and pushes
+2. CI runs automatically
+3. If CI fails: reaction auto-sends fix instructions to agent
+4. If reviewers request changes: reaction auto-sends comments to agent
+5. When approved + green: notify human to merge (unless auto-merge enabled)
+
+### Manual Intervention
+
+When an agent needs human judgment:
+
+1. You'll get a notification (desktop/slack/webhook)
+2. Check the dashboard or `ao status` for details
+3. Attach to the session if needed: `ao session attach <session>`
+4. Send instructions: `ao send <session> '...'`
+5. Or handle the human-only action yourself (merge PR, close issue, etc.) while keeping implementation in worker sessions.
+
+## Tips
+
+1. **Use batch-spawn for multiple issues** - Much faster than spawning one at a time.
+
+2. **Check status before spawning** - Avoid creating duplicate sessions for issues already being worked on.
+
+3. **Let reactions handle routine issues** - CI failures and review comments are auto-forwarded to agents.
+
+4. **Trust the metadata** - Session metadata tracks branch, PR, status, and more for each session.
+
+5. **Use the dashboard for overview** - Terminal for details, dashboard for at-a-glance status.
+
+6. **Cleanup regularly** - `ao session cleanup` removes merged/closed sessions and keeps things tidy.
+
+7. **Monitor the event log** - Full system activity is logged for debugging and auditing.
+
+8. **Don't micro-manage** - Spawn agents, walk away, let notifications bring you back when needed.
+
+{{PROJECT_SPECIFIC_RULES_SECTION_START}}
+
+## Project-Specific Rules
+
+{{projectSpecificRulesSection}}
+{{PROJECT_SPECIFIC_RULES_SECTION_END}}

## Review: `yyovil/fix-issue-1175`

**Summary:** Extracts the orchestrator prompt from inline TypeScript string literals into an external Markdown template (`orchestrator.md`), with a `{{placeholder}}` rendering system. Rollup bundles the `.md` file into the JS output at build time.

### What works well

- **Clean separation of concerns** — prompt content lives in Markdown where it's easy to read and edit, while rendering logic stays in TypeScript.
- **Robust validation** — unresolved placeholders throw, malformed optional section markers throw, prototype property access is guarded via `hasOwnProperty`.
- **Good test coverage** — tests for placeholder expansion, unresolved placeholders, prototype poisoning, malformed sections, and optional section toggling.
- **Build is green**, all 599 tests pass.

### Issues

**1. Uncommitted working tree changes are inconsistent with HEAD**

The working tree has `rollup.config.ts` (TypeScript) replacing `rollup.config.mjs`, and `tsconfig.build.json` now has `emitDeclarationOnly: true` (moved from the CLI flag). But `rollup.config.ts` still has untyped parameters (`importId`, `id`) — since it's now a `.ts` file, this may fail under strict TypeScript checking. These changes should either be committed or reverted to keep the branch clean.

**2. `rollup.config.ts` has implicit `any` parameters**

`getPackageName(importId)` and `rawMarkdown().load(id)` and `external(id)` lack type annotations. The project enforces `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any: error` and strict mode. These will likely fail linting or typecheck depending on how rollup is invoked.

**3. `buildAutomatedReactionsSection` uses a convoluted `String.fromCharCode(42)` for `**`**

```typescript
const markdownBold = String.fromCharCode(42).repeat(2);
```

This is presumably to avoid the markdown bold syntax confusing some tool, but it hurts readability. A simple `"**"` string literal would be clearer — or if there's a real reason for this, it deserves a comment.

**4. `removeOptionalSectionBlocks` only handles exactly 2 hardcoded sections**

The function takes a generic-looking `data` param but the section list is hardcoded. If more optional sections are added, the array must be manually extended. This is fine for now but worth noting.

**5. Minor: em-dash (`—`) changed to hyphen (`-`) in the module docstring and some template text**

The committed diff changes `—` to `-` in places (e.g., "Orchestrator Prompt Generator — generates" → "Orchestrator Prompt Generator - generates"). This is cosmetic but inconsistent with the rest of the codebase which uses em-dashes.

### Verdict

The core refactor is solid and well-tested. The main actionable items are:
1. Commit or revert the uncommitted `.ts` rollup config changes
2. Add type annotations to `rollup.config.ts` if keeping it as TypeScript
3. Consider simplifying the `String.fromCharCode(42)` to `"**"`

# Simplify: Code Review and Cleanup

Review all changed files for reuse, quality, and efficiency. Fix any issues found.

## Phase 1: Identify Changes

Run `git diff` (or `git diff HEAD` if there are staged changes) to see what changed. If there are no git changes, review the most recently modified files that the user mentioned or that you edited earlier in this conversation.

## Phase 2: Launch Three Review Agents in Parallel

Use the Agent tool to launch all three agents concurrently in a single message. Pass each agent the full diff so it has the complete context.

### Agent 1: Code Reuse Review

For each change:

1. **Search for existing utilities and helpers** that could replace newly written code. Look for similar patterns elsewhere in the codebase — common locations are utility directories, shared modules, and files adjacent to the changed ones.
2. **Flag any new function that duplicates existing functionality.** Suggest the existing function to use instead.
3. **Flag any inline logic that could use an existing utility** — hand-rolled string manipulation, manual path handling, custom environment checks, ad-hoc type guards, and similar patterns are common candidates.

### Agent 2: Code Quality Review

Review the same changes for hacky patterns:

1. **Redundant state**: state that duplicates existing state, cached values that could be derived, observers/effects that could be direct calls
2. **Parameter sprawl**: adding new parameters to a function instead of generalizing or restructuring existing ones
3. **Copy-paste with slight variation**: near-duplicate code blocks that should be unified with a shared abstraction
4. **Leaky abstractions**: exposing internal details that should be encapsulated, or breaking existing abstraction boundaries
5. **Stringly-typed code**: using raw strings where constants, enums (string unions), or branded types already exist in the codebase
6. **Unnecessary JSX nesting**: wrapper Boxes/elements that add no layout value — check if inner component props (flexShrink, alignItems, etc.) already provide the needed behavior
7. **Unnecessary comments**: comments explaining WHAT the code does (well-named identifiers already do that), narrating the change, or referencing the task/caller — delete; keep only non-obvious WHY (hidden constraints, subtle invariants, workarounds)

### Agent 3: Efficiency Review

Review the same changes for efficiency:

1. **Unnecessary work**: redundant computations, repeated file reads, duplicate network/API calls, N+1 patterns
2. **Missed concurrency**: independent operations run sequentially when they could run in parallel
3. **Hot-path bloat**: new blocking work added to startup or per-request/per-render hot paths
4. **Recurring no-op updates**: state/store updates inside polling loops, intervals, or event handlers that fire unconditionally — add a change-detection guard so downstream consumers aren't notified when nothing changed. Also: if a wrapper function takes an updater/reducer callback, verify it honors same-reference returns (or whatever the "no change" signal is) — otherwise callers' early-return no-ops are silently defeated
5. **Unnecessary existence checks**: pre-checking file/resource existence before operating (TOCTOU anti-pattern) — operate directly and handle the error
6. **Memory**: unbounded data structures, missing cleanup, event listener leaks
7. **Overly broad operations**: reading entire files when only a portion is needed, loading all items when filtering for one

## Phase 3: Fix Issues

Wait for all three agents to complete. Aggregate their findings and fix each issue directly. If a finding is a false positive or not worth addressing, note it and move on — do not argue with the finding, just skip it.

When done, briefly summarize what was fixed (or confirm the code was already clean).

## Code Reuse Review: `yyovil/fix-issue-1175`

### 1. Reaction-rendering logic duplicated with `prompt-builder.ts`

`orchestrator-prompt.ts` → `buildAutomatedReactionsSection()` iterates `project.reactions` and builds a list of formatted reaction lines. `packages/core/src/prompt-builder.ts` → `buildConfigLayer()` (lines 94–106) does the exact same loop over `project.reactions`, filtering on `reaction.auto && reaction.action === "send-to-agent"`. The new code adds a second branch for `action === "notify"` and uses bold formatting, but the core iteration and filter logic is a copy. If the `ProjectConfig.reactions` shape changes, both places must be updated.

**Recommendation:** Extract a shared `describeReactions(project: ProjectConfig): string[]` utility (or re-use `buildConfigLayer`'s structure) rather than maintaining two loops.

### 2. `String.fromCharCode(42)` workaround for `**`

In `buildAutomatedReactionsSection` (line 32–33), bold markdown is produced via:
```typescript
const markdownBold = String.fromCharCode(42).repeat(2);
```
This is the only place in the entire codebase that uses this pattern. It appears to be a gitleaks/linter workaround, but it is opaque and fragile. There is no shared helper for this, and no other prompt-building code needs it. It should either be a named constant (`const BOLD = "**"`) in a shared location, or the workaround should be documented with a comment explaining why the direct literal is avoided.

### 3. `rawMarkdown` Vite/Rollup plugin duplicated

The `rawMarkdown` plugin body is copy-pasted verbatim between two files:

- `/packages/core/rollup.config.ts` (lines 20–31)
- `/packages/core/vitest.config.ts` (lines 10–21)

The only difference is that `vitest.config.ts` adds `enforce: "pre"`. Both read `id.endsWith(".md")` and return `export default ${JSON.stringify(...)}`. Since both files live in the same package, this could be extracted to a shared local file, e.g., `packages/core/build-plugins.ts`, exported as `rawMarkdown()`, and imported by both configs. This is low-risk since both configs are in the same package directory.

### 4. Rollup is unique to `core` — no sharing opportunity across packages

Every other package uses plain `tsc` as its build script. `@aoagents/ao-core` is the only package with a rollup config. No cross-package rollup sharing opportunity exists or is needed; the new `rollup.config.ts` is appropriately scoped to core.

### 5. The template rendering system is novel — no existing utility to reuse

There is no existing `{{placeholder}}`-style template engine anywhere in the codebase. The CLI's `generateRulesFromTemplates()` in `/packages/cli/src/lib/project-detection.ts` reads markdown files via `readFileSync` at runtime (using `__dirname` path resolution), but it performs simple string concatenation with no placeholder substitution. The `renderTemplate` / `removeOptionalSectionBlocks` functions in the new file are genuinely new logic and cannot be replaced by an existing utility.

### 6. CLI markdown templates are loaded at runtime, not bundled

`/packages/cli/src/lib/project-detection.ts` (lines 125–158) reads `.md` files from `packages/cli/templates/rules/` at runtime using `readFileSync` + `join(__dirname, ...)`. The new `orchestrator-prompt.ts` takes the opposite approach: it bundles the `.md` file at build time via rollup. These are two different patterns for the same problem (shipping prompt text with a package). The inconsistency is worth noting — if `core` ever needs more prompt templates, both approaches are now in play in the monorepo with no documented policy for which to prefer.

### Summary Table

| Finding | Severity | File(s) |
|---|---|---|
| Reactions loop duplicated in `prompt-builder.ts` | Medium | `orchestrator-prompt.ts:31-48`, `prompt-builder.ts:94-106` |
| `rawMarkdown` plugin duplicated | Low | `rollup.config.ts:20-31`, `vitest.config.ts:10-21` |
| `String.fromCharCode(42)` opaque workaround | Low | `orchestrator-prompt.ts:32-33` |
| Runtime `readFileSync` vs. bundled `.md` inconsistency | Low | `orchestrator-prompt.ts`, `cli/src/lib/project-detection.ts:124-168` |

## Efficiency Review: `orchestrator-prompt.ts`

### 1. `String.fromCharCode(42).repeat(2)` — Obscured Constant, Zero Gain

**Location:** `buildAutomatedReactionsSection`, line 32.

`String.fromCharCode(42).repeat(2)` produces `"**"`. This is evaluated fresh on every call to the function (which is called on every `generateOrchestratorPrompt` invocation). The obfuscation provides no value and hides intent. It is not a hot loop, but it is pointless work. A module-level `const BOLD = "**"` would be cleaner and avoids repeated evaluation.

### 2. `removeOptionalSectionBlocks` Walks the Template Twice Per Section Marker

**Location:** `removeOptionalSectionBlocks`, lines 80-81.

For each optional section, `indexOf` is called twice on `interpolated` — once for the start marker and once for the end marker. With two sections today, that is four passes over a ~5 KB string. Because the sections are processed sequentially and the string is mutated between iterations, a combined regex that captures start-marker/content/end-marker in one pass would halve the scans. This is minor at the current template size but will degrade linearly if more optional sections are added.

### 3. `renderTemplate` Does a Full-String Regex Scan After Optional-Section Processing Has Already Run

**Location:** `generateOrchestratorPrompt`, lines 152-156.

`removeOptionalSectionBlocks` already iterates the string with `indexOf`. Then `renderTemplate` does a second full-pass `String.prototype.replace` with a regex over the same (now-modified) string. These two passes are inherently sequential and unavoidable given the design, but the template pre-processing and the variable substitution could be collapsed into a single regex pass if the section markers were redesigned to use a different syntax (e.g. `<!--...-->` wrappers). As written, the two-pass approach is acceptable but worth noting.

### 4. `normalizeRenderedPrompt` Is a Third Full-String Pass

**Location:** `normalizeRenderedPrompt`, line 142, called in `generateOrchestratorPrompt`.

After optional-section removal and variable substitution, a third regex pass collapses consecutive blank lines. The need for this pass is a direct consequence of optional sections leaving behind double blank lines when removed. The template itself could be designed to avoid producing triple newlines in the empty-section case (e.g. placing the section markers on the same line as adjacent content rather than on blank-separated lines). Eliminating the blank-line artifact at the template level would remove this entire pass.

### 5. `hasRenderDataKey` Uses `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call` Inside a Replace Callback

**Location:** `hasRenderDataKey` / `renderTemplate`, lines 108-138.

`hasOwnProperty.call` is invoked once per matched placeholder. The `data` object is a plain object literal created by `createRenderData` — it is not a prototype-hostile object and cannot have inherited keys that shadow own keys. Using `key in data` or `Object.hasOwn(data, key)` (available since Node 16.9) would be equivalent, less verbose, and marginally faster since it avoids the cross-object method lookup. This is a micro-concern but aligns with the rest of the codebase style.

### 6. Call-Site Frequency: `generateOrchestratorPrompt` Is a Cold-Path, One-Shot Function

The function is called exactly twice in production paths — once at `ao start` time (`packages/cli/src/commands/start.ts:1064`) and once per HTTP POST to `/api/orchestrators` (`packages/web/src/app/api/orchestrators/route.ts:67`). Both are extremely infrequent (spawning an orchestrator is a human-initiated, once-per-project-session action). None of the efficiency issues identified are hot-path problems.

### 7. Build Config: `rawMarkdown` Plugin Reads the File on Every Rollup Invocation

**Location:** `rollup.config.ts`, lines 20-31.

The `rawMarkdown` plugin calls `readFile(id, "utf8")` inside the `load` hook. This is fine — Rollup calls `load` once per module per build invocation, and the result is inlined as a string literal in the bundle. At runtime there is no I/O; the template is a module-level constant. No issue here.

### 8. No Memoization Needed — But Worth Documenting

`generateOrchestratorPrompt` is pure (deterministic given its inputs). If the same `(config, projectId, project)` triple were passed repeatedly — for example, if the web API route were polled — memoizing the result would be beneficial. Given the current call pattern (one-shot spawn), memoization would be premature optimization. However, if a future caller (e.g. a preview/diff endpoint) calls this in a loop, the absence of a cache will become visible.

---

### Summary Table

| Issue | Severity | Location |
|---|---|---|
| `String.fromCharCode(42).repeat(2)` repeated on every call | Cosmetic | `buildAutomatedReactionsSection` L32 |
| Two `indexOf` scans per optional section instead of one regex | Minor | `removeOptionalSectionBlocks` L80-81 |
| Triple full-string passes (section removal → variable sub → normalize) | Minor, structural | `generateOrchestratorPrompt` L152-156 |
| Blank-line normalization pass forced by template blank-line layout | Minor, avoidable at template level | `normalizeRenderedPrompt` / `orchestrator.md` L151,158,213,218 |
| `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call` instead of `Object.hasOwn` | Cosmetic | `hasRenderDataKey` L112 |

None of these rise to a blocking level given the cold-path call frequency. The most impactful structural note is that the triple-pass design (`removeOptionalSectionBlocks` → `renderTemplate` → `normalizeRenderedPrompt`) could be compressed into fewer passes, and the trailing-blank-line normalization is a symptom of the template's formatting rather than a necessity.
